expressions.english: to hand-make something
expressions.notes: 손 = hand

expressions.english: to make (a story) into a book
expressions.notes: 책 = book

expressions.english: to make food
expressions.notes: 음식 = food

expressions.english: Do you know how to make this?
expressions.notes:
이거 = this thing; this
-(으)ㄹ 줄 알다 = to know how to + V

expressions.english: Let me know once you are done making it.
expressions.notes:
다 = all; every (thing)
다-(으)면 = once + S + finish/done + V-ing
말하다 = to talk; to say; to speak; to tell
-이/어/여 주세요 = (polite) Please do + something + for me.

expressions.english: Who made this?
expressions.notes: 누가 = who; someone (subject)

expressions.english:
It's handmade.
I made it with my hands.
expressions.notes: 그거 = that

expressions.english: I can't make such a thing.
expressions.notes:
저 = (polite) I; me
그런 = (something) like that; such + noun
못 = can't

expressions.english: I made this myself.
expressions.notes:
제 = (polite) my; I
직접 = directly; by oneself; with one's own hands

expressions.english: I made a new business card recently.
expressions.notes:
이번에 = this time; recently
새 = new
명함 = business card

expressions.english: I tried making some tteokbokki with my friend.
expressions.notes:
친구 = friend
떡볶이 = tteokbokki; boiled rice cakes and fish cakes in a spicy sauce
-어/어/여 보다 = to try + V-ing

expressions.english: How did you make it so quickly?
expressions.notes:
어떻게 = how
이렇게 = like this, in this manner; so +adjective
빠르다 = to be fast
빨리 is an adverb form of 빠르다.

expressions.english: Tell me how to make this.
expressions.notes:
방법 = method, way
좀 = a little; please
알려주다 = to let someone know; to teach something to someone
-(으)세요 = (polite) imperative

expressions.korean: 제가 만든 김치 볶음밥 맛이 어때요?
expressions.english: How does the kimchi fried rice that I made taste?
expressions.notes:
김치 볶음밥 = kimchi fried rice
맛 = taste
어때요? = how is + N?; used to suggest, propose, or offer something

expressions.english: They made his story into a book.
expressions.notes:
그 = the; that
사람 = person, people
이야기 = story; what one says

expressions.korean: 어제 찍은 사진으로 비디오를 만들었어 요.
expressions.english: I made a video with the photos I took yesterday.
expressions.notes:
어제 = yesterday
(사진을) 찍다 = to take (photos)
사진 = photo
비디오 = video

expressions.korean: 제가 실수를 해서 괜히 일을 더 만들었 어요.
expressions.english:
I made a mistake and created more work.
I made a silly mistake and made the task more daunting.
expressions.notes:
실수 = mistake
실수를 하다 = to make a mistake
-어/어/여서 = because; since
괜히 = needlessly; in vain; uselessly
일 = work; task; thing; stuff; occasion
더 = more
일을 만들다 = to make trouble
일을 더 만들다 = to make the work/trouble bigger

expressions.korean: 음식을 먹을 줄은 아는데 만들 줄은 몰 라요.
expressions.english: I know how to eat food, but don't know how to make it.
expressions.notes: -(으)ㄹ 줄 모르다 = to not know how to + V

expressions.korean: 오늘 저녁에 손님이 오셔서 음식을 많 이 만들어야 해요.
expressions.english: There will be some guests coming this evening, so I have to make a lot of food.
expressions.notes:
오늘 = today
저녁 = evening; dinner
손님 = guest; visitor
오시다 = (honorific) to come
많다 = to be a lot
많이 is an adverb form of 많다.
-어/어/여야 하다 = should; have to; must

expressions.korean: 제가 제일 좋아하는 감독이 이번에 새 영화를 만들었어요.
expressions.english: My favorite director recently made a new movie.
expressions.notes:
제일 = the best; the most
좋아하다 = to like
감독 = director
영화 = movie
